title: ITU AI for Good Robotics Youth Challenge Uganda — Building Next-Generation Problem Solvers
detail: The ITU's AI for Good initiative hosted its Robotics for Good Youth Challenge in Uganda on April 17, gathering young people to design and build AI-powered robotic solutions to local real-world problems. The event is part of ITU's global effort to build AI literacy and robotics skills among youth in the Global South, addressing the gap in AI talent development outside of North America, Europe, and East Asia. Teams competed to solve challenges in agriculture, health delivery, and environmental monitoring — applying robotics and machine learning to problems directly relevant to Ugandan communities. These youth challenges form a core part of the AI for Good strategy to ensure the next generation of AI innovators includes voices from Africa, building a pipeline of AI talent who will develop tools addressing local needs rather than importing solutions designed for different contexts.

media.caption: ITU Robotics for Good Youth Challenge Uganda — building AI skills among African youth

title: Global Radiology AI Market Reaches $760M — Projected to Triple to $2.27B by 2030 as Diagnostic AI Matures
detail: Industry research released April 17 projects the global radiology AI market to grow from $760 million in 2025 to $2.27 billion by 2030 at a 24.5% compound annual growth rate, reflecting accelerating adoption of AI diagnostic tools in hospital systems worldwide. The growth is driven by multi-modal platform integration (AI tools that read across CT, MRI, X-ray, and pathology), deeper PACS/EHR connectivity that embeds AI assistance directly into radiologist workflows, and increasing OEM partnerships with imaging equipment manufacturers. For global health equity, the implications are significant: AI-powered radiology platforms are increasingly being deployed in lower-resource settings where specialist radiologist shortages are acute — a single AI platform can handle screening volume equivalent to multiple full-time radiologists, potentially transforming diagnostic access in Africa and South Asia where many district hospitals lack any on-site radiologist. The market trajectory also validates the long-term commercial sustainability of AI diagnostic models in healthcare systems.

media.caption: Radiology AI market projected at $2.27B by 2030, driving AI diagnostic access in under-resourced hospitals globally
